data.title: Court imposes freezing order on Autobahn group companies, directors amid allegations of fraud and bad faith
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] Two directors and five companies of the Autobahn Rent A Car group (Autobahn) were served a freezing order by SingaporeвҖҷs High Court on Jan 9, as creditors allege fraud and the failure to disclose the location of vehicles under loans.

Directors Tan Boon Kee вҖ“ also known as Roy Tan вҖ“ and Sanjay Kumar Rai are subject to the injunction up to a limit of S$101.9 million each.

The five companies under the injunction are Autobahn Rent A Car, AhTan Car Repairs, Hamilton Autobahn, Hamilton Autohub and Hamilton Capital, each subject to varying limits of up to S$54.5 million.

data.title: Singapore turns to next-generation power systems to scale AI, train future workforce
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] As artificial intelligence (AI) drives rising energy demand, data-centre operator ST Telemedia Global Data Centres (STT GDC) is deploying more efficient power systems to scale its operations, through a pilot it described as being the first in South-east Asia.

Dubbed the FutureGrid Accelerator, the high-voltage direct current (HVDC) AI test bed was launched on Monday (Jan 26) at the Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Electrification and Power Grids Centre on Jurong Island.

The move comes as Singapore moves towards a low-carbon energy grid while advancing South-east AsiaГўВҖВҷs energy transition and net-zero ambitions.

Lim Mingcheng, country head for Singapore at STT GDC, noted that traditional alternating-current systems ГўВҖВңare hitting their physical and efficiency limitsГўВҖВқ.

As AI model training increases, the power demand for server racks also rises. Currently, a typical server rack draws about 20 kilowatts of power ГўВҖВ“ equal to the monthly power usage of 30 five-room HDB flats.

Lim said that by 2030, companies such as Nvidia could push this demand up to 1 megawatt (MW), or enough to power about 1,500 five-room flats for a month.

ГўВҖВңSo, the question is not whether this growth will happen, but rather, how (we can) support this sustainably,ГўВҖВқ he added.

With the new HVDC infrastructure, data centres will be able to support ultra-high-density racks exceeding 1 MW, with higher reliability and lower costs.

HVDC power systems will also create energy savings of up to 30 per cent from their alternating-current counterparts, while using 45 per cent less copper and having a power-infrastructure footprint that is smaller by up to 40 per cent.

Up to 400 tonnes of carbon emissions will be reduced per MW annually. Lim noted that this is roughly equivalent to 16,000 trees a year across a single large data-centre facility.

The pilot will run through this year. STT GDC hopes to deploy this technology in future data centres in Singapore and progressively scale it within South-east Asia over the next few years.

Talent development

ГўВҖВңYou will also need the people with the skills to operate (advanced AI infrastructure),ГўВҖВқ said Lim.

He noted that many companies still face a capability gap between their strategic ambition and their ability to execute AI workloads at an operational level. This gap, he added, is especially pronounced for emerging technologies such as HVDC.

To address this, STT GDC signed multiple memorandums of understanding with the Institute of Technical Education, Singapore Polytechnic, NTU and the National University of Singapore.

In total, more than 8,000 Singaporeans will benefit from industry-aligned training in AI infrastructure and sustainable energy systems over the next five years.

Key initiatives proposed include internship opportunities, sponsorships as well as graduate pathways.

STT GDC said these will ensure that the ГўВҖВңscaling of HVDC ГўВҖВ“ and the broader energy transition ГўВҖВ“ is matched by the transformation and availability of skills, jobs and talent development pathwaysГўВҖВқ.

data.title: Singapore factory activity rises 8.3% in December, beating expectations
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] The RepublicГўВҖВҷs factory output increased 8.3 per cent year on year in December, a sharp moderation from the previous monthГўВҖВҷs 18.2 per cent rise.

Still, DecemberГўВҖВҷs industrial production growth beat private-sector economistsГўВҖВҷ median expectations of 7.4 per cent rise, a poll by Bloomberg indicated.

Excluding the volatile biomedical cluster, manufacturing output grew 16 per cent year on year, a jump from NovemberГўВҖВҷs 9.1 per cent increase.

On a seasonally adjusted month on month basis, manufacturing output fell 13.3 per cent in December, in contrast to NovemberГўВҖВҷs 7.8 per cent contraction.

Cluster performance

Most clusters recorded growth in December. Factory output for the electronics cluster grew 30.8 per cent year on year, led by the semiconductors segment which grew 32.4 per cent on ГўВҖВңsustained and strongГўВҖВқ demand for artificial intelligence-related products.

The transport engineering cluster grew 19.9 per cent, supported by growth in the aerospace, as well as marine and offshore segment.

Precision engineering grew 3.4 per cent in December on the year, on the back of growth from the precision modules and components segment.

Meanwhile, general manufacturing remained unchanged.

In contrast, the chemicals cluster declined 1.6 per cent year on year, with growth in the specialities and petroleum segments offset by declines in the petrochemicals segment.

Biomedical manufacturing tumbled 38.8 per cent, dragged by a contraction in the pharmaceuticals segment which fell 69.7 per cent.

data.title: PBOC pledges stronger China-Hong Kong market ties
data.description:
[HONG KONG] China will promote greater financial market connectivity between the mainland and Hong Kong, Deputy Governor Zou Lan of the PeopleГўВҖВҷs Bank of China (PBOC) said on Monday (Jan 26).

Speaking at the Asia Finance Forum in Hong Kong, Zou said the central bank would support and advance the development of the cityГўВҖВҷs offshore yuan market and push for the launch of yuan-denominated government bond futures in Hong Kong.

China will support the Hong Kong Monetary AuthorityГўВҖВҷs move to double yuan funding under last yearГўВҖВҷs swap facility to 200ГўВҖВҜ billion yuan (S$36.4 billion), Zou said.

China will increase offshore issuance of yuan-denominated government bonds to boost liquidity and meet rising demand from overseas investors for high-quality yuan assets, Zou said

China will also broaden liquidity-management and hedging tools for foreign investors, expand interest-rate and currency derivatives, and push for the listing of yuan government bond futures in Hong Kong, he added.

Measures taken in recent months to deepen financial cooperation with the Asian financial hub include the launch of cross-border Payment Connect and expansion of the Bond Connect scheme to allow more onshore institutions to invest offshore. REUTERS

data.title: India-EU ГўВҖВҳmother-of-all dealsГўВҖВҷ accelerated by USГўВҖВҷ protectionist agenda
data.description:
CONVENTIONAL wisdom holds that Donald TrumpГўВҖВҷs US presidencies have fuelled global protectionism. Yet, as much as his ГўВҖВңAmerica FirstГўВҖВқ agenda triggered a proliferation of measures such as US tariffs, it has indirectly spurred other nations to push forward with major new trade deals.

Since TrumpГўВҖВҷs re-election in November 2024, the evidence has mounted. Take the example of the 27-member EUГўВҖВҷs trade agreement with the Mercosur bloc (Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay and Uruguay) and Indonesia in Asean.

Beyond this, Brussels is negotiating with the Gulf Cooperation Council states in the Middle East and signalling closer engagement with the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership bloc, which includes multiple Asean states such as Singapore.

data.title: SoftBank stops talks to buy data centre firm Switch
data.description:
TOKYO] SoftBank Group has halted talks about an acquisition of US data centre operator Switch, a setback to founder Masayoshi SonГўВҖВҷs ambition to roll out Stargate AI infrastructure, according to people familiar with the matter.

For months, Son pursued a deal of around US$50 billion for Switch, convinced that direct control of the latterГўВҖВҷs network of energy-efficient data centres would help the US$500 billion Stargate push to generate computing power for partner OpenAI.ГӮВ 

But earlier this month, Son conceded that a full acquisition was off the table and scrapped a planned January announcement, the people said, asking not to be named because the matter is private. The two sides remain in active discussions about a partial investment or a partnership, they said. Earlier this month, SoftBank inked a US$3 billion deal to buy New York-listed investment firm DigitalBridge Group, which holds a majority stake in Switch.ГӮВ 

A SoftBank spokesperson did not respond to a request for comment. Representatives of Switch and DigitalBridge declined to comment.

The deal would have been one of the Japanese companyГўВҖВҷs biggest to date, and would have given a much-needed boost to the Stargate Project to build US data centres. Son had seen that project as a key path to play a bigger role in an artificial intelligence race and had pledged to deploy US$100 billion ГўВҖВңimmediatelyГўВҖВқ alongside OpenAI, Oracle and Abu DhabiГўВҖВҷs MGX.ГӮВ 

Some within the SoftBank camp had been wary about the size of the deal as well as the logistics of running data centre campuses from Las Vegas to Atlanta, the people said. Switch is simultaneously preparing for an initial public offering as soon as this year, and the companyГўВҖВҷs backers have considered a valuation of about US$60 billion including debt via a stock-market listing. Any deal would also likely come under scrutiny from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the US.

While itГўВҖВҷs unlikely that SoftBank and Switch will revive talks about a full acquisition, the two sides may find a way in the future, given SonГўВҖВҷs keen interest in a deal. The billionaire has said he eyed Arm Holdings for years before he was able to acquire the UK chip designer in 2016. SoftBank also owns an Ohio manufacturing facility that is operated by TaiwanГўВҖВҷs Hon Hai Precision Industry, which might be a model for a partnership with Switch.

Despite being early to invest in AI technologies, Son and SoftBank have largely missed out on a global rush to build the semiconductors, server racks and other hardware to support machine learning, with the lionГўВҖВҷs share of money flowing to a small circle of chipmakers including Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.ГӮВ 

SoftBank has doubled down on its bets in AI in recent months. Over the past year, the Tokyo-based company has amassed an 11 per cent stake in OpenAI, injecting US$22.5 billion just last month. It bought US chip designer Ampere Computing for US$6.5 billion and announced a US$5.4 billion acquisition of ABBГўВҖВҷs robotics unit. To finance some of that cost, SoftBank has sold down its T-Mobile US shares, unloaded its entire Nvidia stake and expanded a margin loan using its Arm shares.

SoftBankГўВҖВҷs investments in AI, along with the sharp drop in the value of Arm shares at the end of last year, are heaping pressure on its creditworthiness, S&P Global Ratings warned earlier this month. While SoftBank has a record of discipline with managing its finances, ГўВҖВңif it does not take prompt easing measures, such as liquidation of held assets, pressure will intensify on the credit ratings,ГўВҖВқ analysts Kei Ishikawa and Makiko Yoshimura wrote in a report.ГӮВ BLOOMBERG

data.title: Blackstone eyes more hiring in Asia to tap private market demand
data.description:
[HONG KONG] Blackstone is planning to hire more people across Asia to tap growing opportunities in private markets, said Ed Huang, the firmвҖҷs head of Asia-Pacific private wealth.

The alternative asset manager sees huge potential from bringing private markets to individual investors, who are looking for alternatives to the conventional portfolio of stocks and bonds, Huang said on Monday (Jan 26).

вҖңWe have roughly doubled our team size over the last two years,вҖқ Huang said. вҖңWe are certainly continuing to grow our business. We want to expand in Japan. We have added people on the ground in Australia. We are putting people on the ground in Korea. We are looking to continue to scale up our team to meet the opportunity.вҖқ

Investment managers are increasingly eager to capture money flows from high-net-worth individuals, in large part because these investors still have negligible exposure to private equity and private credit. While institutions allocate roughly 30 per cent of their capital to private market products, individual investors commit only around 3 per cent, said Huang.

Blackstone has been ramping up its presence in Asia, part of a broader push by global rivals including Bain Capital and KKR. These firms have occasionally competed over the same deals: All three joined a recent bidding race for Japanese personal-care group FineToday Holdings, although Blackstone and KKR ultimately backed away.

Among key investment ideas, the US manager continues to bet on major themes including artificial intelligence, energy and life sciences, Huang said. BLOOMBERG

data.title: British banks to join European rivals in hiking profit targets, sources say
data.description:
[LONDON] BritainвҖҷs biggest banks, including HSBC and NatWest, are set to follow their European rivals and lift their key profit targets when they report annual earnings in the coming weeks, sources close to the matter said.

HSBC is expected to raise its return on tangible equity (Rote) outlook вҖ“ a key measure of profitability вҖ“ above current guidance of вҖңmid-teens or betterвҖқ, while NatWest is likely to upgrade its guidance for 2027, currently at 15 per cent, to as much as 17 per cent, two sources said.

Barclays, which in October said it expected an Rote of 12 per cent or above in 2026, should also lift its targets, a third source familiar with the lender said.

Analysts have also said that they believe Barclays and HSBC can raise their targets by as much as 200 basis points when they set out guidance for the coming years. They report their earnings on Feb 10 and Feb 25, respectively.

In continental Europe, many banks have already lifted their profit goals, signalling confidence higher margins will last for years.

Increased profitability targets show banks expect to keep benefiting from benign interest rate conditions and continued loan and fee income growth, although aiming higher is not without risks and can leave investors disappointed if economies stutter.

Lloyds Banking Group could also lift its targets this year, aiming for Rote to rise to as much as 18.5 per cent by 2028 from this yearвҖҷs goal of more than 15 per cent, analysts at Jefferies said this month.

The banks all declined to comment.

вҖңUK banks have benefited from earnings resilience lasting longer than initially expected, supported by higher interest rates, robust credit quality and tighter cost control,вҖқ said Peter Rothwell, head of banking at KPMG UK.

Lloyds and Deutsche Bank report full-year earnings on Thursday (Jan 29), kicking off the European bank reporting season following a bumper set of numbers on Wall Street.

Banks across europe pushing profits higher

After years of poor profitability and share performance following the financial crisis, European banking stocks have more than doubled since early 2024 and risen 60 per cent in the past year, far outpacing US banks.

Among European rivals, Spanish banks Santander and BBVA have grown income while keeping costs under control, raising expectations for improved targets.

JPMorgan expects BBVA to have delivered around 20 per cent Rote in 2025, broadly in line with 2024, with profitability rising to 22 per cent in 2026 and reaching 26 per cent by 2028.

Santander could target a Rote by 2028 of around 19 to 20 per cent, up from 16.1 per cent as at September, Barclays analysts said. GermanyвҖҷs Deutsche Bank in November set a new Rote target for 2028 of greater than 13 per cent, up from its 2025 target of 10 per cent.

Analysts expect Deutsche to confirm it met the 2025 target, alongside figures that could show its biggest profit since 2007. Volatile markets and a flurry of corporate deals should also lift investment bank earnings, buoying the likes of Deutsche, Barclays and UBS, after most Wall Street banks reported rising revenues and a bullish outlook.

FranceвҖҷs Societe Generale, BNP Paribas and Credit Agricole may buck the trend as higher costs and domestic competition weigh on profits, analysts said. REUTERS

data.title: Singapore dollar hits 11-year high against greenback
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] The Singapore dollar strengthened to its strongest level against the greenback since late 2014 on Monday (Jan 26).

The USD/SGD currency pair slumped to 1.2679 during trading hours on Monday, at 10.40 am Singapore time. Conversely, the Singdollar strengthened to around 0.7887 against the greenback.

By 1.13pm, the USD/SGD pair pared some of its losses to 1.2685, and SGD/USD was at 0.7883.

US declines

The greenback faced fresh headwinds amid market chatter that the US might coordinate with Japan on currency intervention. Rumours of the joint intervention have weighed heavily on the US dollar, dragging down the US dollar index to 97.09 as at 11.15 am.

The USD suffered its worst weekly decline last week since May 2025, driven first by US President TrumpвҖҷs sudden Taco (вҖңTrump Always Chickens OutвҖқ) on threatened tariffs against the EU over Greenland.

Later, a sharp USD/JPY pullback on suspected interventions spilled over into broader USD selling, analysts from DBS said in a Monday note.

On Sunday, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said that the Japanese government would вҖңtake necessary steps against speculative or abnormal market movesвҖқ when asked about the weaknesses in the Japanese government bonds market and the yen in a talk show.

вҖңHowever, there has been no confirmation of actual intervention, which could limit downside follow-through in USD/JPY,вҖқ DBS analysts said.

They expect the Fed to pause at its Jan 27-28 Federal Open Market Committee meeting, following three consecutive rate cuts.

Meanwhile, UOB analysts said in a Jan 26 note on USD/SGD: вҖңWe did not anticipate the sharp selloff that sent USD plunging to a low of 1.2715. USD continues to weaken on the open today.вҖқ

вҖңImpulsive downward momentum suggests further USD weakness, but deeply oversold conditions suggest any decline could be limited to a test of 1.2675,вҖқ they add.

Domestic strength

The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) is in a вҖңcomfortableвҖқ position to maintain its current appreciation policy, and is widely expected to leave its exchange-rate settings вҖ“ the slope, width and mid-point of the policy band вҖ“ unchanged at its upcoming review on Thursday, analysts from Maybank said in a Monday note.

вҖңMAS should be in a relatively comfortable position (in contrast to the Fed) to maintain current policy stance of slight (0.5 per cent per annum) appreciation, which should provide support for the Singdollar,вҖқ they said.

In contrast to the fiscal uncertainties plaguing other developed markets, DBS analysts noted in a Monday note that Singapore Government Securities have outperformed global peers, attracting вҖңflight-to-qualityвҖқ inflows from investors wary of the fiscal risks in the US and Europe.

Maybank analysts echoed this view, citing the firmness of the trade-weighted Singdollar as вҖңevidence of SingaporeвҖҷs safe-haven appealвҖқ.

Maybank noted that with the SingdollarвҖҷs rally, the Singapore dollar nominal effective exchange rate (S$NEER) is trading at about 1.89 per cent above the implied mid-point of the policy band.

This places the currency near the top of its estimated trading range. Maybank analysts warned that вҖңfurther sharp moves to the topside would raise the risk of interventionвҖқ by the central bank.

DBS sees the currency pair hitting a technical floor. The bank's model estimates that as the trade-weighted Singdollar is just 0.25 per cent away from hitting the policy bandвҖҷs ceiling, the USD/SGD slide is likely limited to 1.2675.

Projecting UOBвҖҷs view over the next one to three weeks, analysts said: вҖңThe price action continues to suggest that USD remains weak. The next level to watch is 1.2650.вҖқ

data.title: Operation Hope Foundation tackles poverty in Cambodia and Nepal, while doubling down on fraud
data.description:
[SINGAPORE] Robert Kee, founder of Operation Hope Foundation (OHF), said that some big organisations like to play the вҖңnumbers gameвҖқ. For example, they could donate US$1 million to multiple beneficiaries, which could result in a recipient getting only 33 per cent of direct benefit.

But OHF does things вҖңdifferentlyвҖқ, said Kee. The foundation ensures that its beneficiaries receive at least 80 per cent of its funding directly.

OHF manages to maintain this level of funding by running key programmes and interacting with beneficiaries on the ground, with processes in place to build accountability and prevent fraud.В 

Since its establishment in 2001, the foundation has spent around S$6.2 million on its projects to tackle poverty in rural villages in Cambodia and Nepal. In 2024 alone, OHF spent around S$916,000.В 

Kee said that people often ask him why his charity does not work with beneficiaries in Singapore. After travelling to Cambodia more than 25 years ago and seeing how villagers lived, he realised the needs were greater there.

Key programmes

OHF runs a childrenвҖҷs home in both Cambodia and Nepal. The home in Cambodia вҖ“ which spans 80,000 square feet (sq ft) вҖ“ serves 105 children, while the one in Nepal serves 55.В 

Kee called them a вҖңseven-star childrenвҖҷs homeвҖқ, with each comprising a dormitory, cafe, dining hall, multipurpose hall, classroom and sports area.

вҖңMany orphanages are just houses filled with children, not a complex designed specifically for children,вҖқ the 78-year-old added.

When the children are not at school, they participate in activities to build skills such as sport and cooking. They also learn how to run food stalls for volunteer visits and earn pocket money, which they can save and take with them when they move out after they turn 18.

In addition, in Cambodia, OHF staff visit villages to identify people who could benefit from a new house. They target families with household income of less than US$100, and take into account their education level and living conditions.

Kee noted that Cambodia is one of the few countries where people still live in thatched houses, which have a shaky foundation as well as no doors or windows.

Instead, OHF builds houses from scratch using wood вҖ“ which is more suitable for CambodiaвҖҷs weather, as it is cooling and more durable.

The charity does not hire contractors; its staff train volunteers to construct the house, as well as a toilet and well. As a result, the organisation can control the building process and materials instead of going through a third party.

So far, the non-profit has built nearly 400 houses. These not only provide shelter, but have also allowed some beneficiaries to start small businesses as they now have privacy and better equipment.

In December 2025, Kee opened the Himalayan Hideaway Resort Pokhara, The Centara Collection. This hotel in Nepal has 42 rooms, as well as a spa, pool and multiple eateries. He called it the вҖңoptimal solutionвҖқ for beneficiaries, as it provides jobs to local communities.В 

Kee personally paid 23 farmers for the land вҖ“ spanning 400,000 sq ft вҖ“ and found вҖңgoodвҖқ local partners to build this resort, he said, which took six years to complete.

Preventing corruption

He noted that other charities with operations in foreign countries usually have local non-governmental organisations (NGOs) running the programmes, instead of doing it themselves, like OHF does.В 

вҖңThese charities just do the overall financing, and they have local NGOs execute projects. But the local NGOs (can be) corrupt,вҖқ he observed.

When there are so many intermediaries involved, Kee said, the direct impact to the beneficiary is small because each intermediary takes a cut of the funds.

вҖңYour money can do a lot, but you must make sure itвҖҷs not siphoned off along the way.вҖқ

For every project, OHF records each item used вҖ“ even down to a zinc nail used when constructing a toilet. Kee and OHF staff also monitor projects on the ground, or via Zoom when they are in Singapore.В 

The founder noted that he adopts a вҖңdevil in the detailsвҖқ mindset, which helps to prevent fraud.

вҖңSuperficially everything looks fine, but when you look at the details, you can identify problems,вҖқ he said. вҖңYou must have the assumption that fraud will occur. If you know your local sources and costs, you can compare the prices of what youвҖҷre buying.вҖқ

Kee вҖ“ who founded the small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) Radac and Applied Digital System вҖ“ emphasised the importance of justifying costs. вҖңAs an SME (owner), I am very cost-conscious because my profit margin is very low.вҖқ

With such processes in place, he noted that charities will not be as prone to fraud and their benefit-to-cost ratio will increase.

Between 2015 and 2024, more than 2,000 volunteers from nearly 250 teams вҖ“ from companies, schools and churches вҖ“ participated in OHFвҖҷs community projects, whether it was in the childrenвҖҷs homes or building houses.В 

вҖңWhat can you do in four days thatвҖҷs really meaningful? You can build a house for a family (and) make a significant difference in their lives,вҖқ he said.
